tool_name: Roof Truss Member Force & Joint Reaction — Simple Warren / Pratt Configuration

inputs.name: Dead Load — Roof Cladding + Self-Weight (g_k)
inputs.note: Metal deck + insulation ≈ 0.4–0.7 kN/m²; concrete slab ≈ 3–5 kN/m²

inputs.name: Imposed / Snow Load (q_k)
inputs.note: EN 1991-1-3 snow or ASCE 7-22 §7 — regional ground snow × shape factor

inputs.name: Design Wind Uplift Pressure (w_u)
inputs.note: Negative (uplift) wind pressure; ASCE 7-22 §27 or EN 1991-1-4 Cp,e

formulas.name: Factored Panel Point Load (P_u) — LRFD / ULS
formulas.expression: w_dead_kn_m = dead_load_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m; w_live_kn_m = live_load_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m; w_factored_kn_m = 1.2 * w_dead_kn_m + 1.6 * w_live_kn_m; panel_length_m = truss_span_m / num_panels; P_u_kn = w_factored_kn_m * panel_length_m

formulas.name: End Reactions (R_A = R_B) — Symmetric Loading
formulas.reference: Simple truss with symmetric loading: R = n_panel_points × P_u / 2

formulas.name: Maximum Bottom Chord Tension (F_BChord) — Mid-Span
formulas.expression: M_max_knm = R_kn * truss_span_m / 2 - P_u_kn * panel_length_m * num_panels / 4; F_BChord_kn = M_max_knm / truss_height_m
formulas.reference: Method of sections: F_BC = M_max / h; maximum mid-span moment for symmetric loading

formulas.name: Maximum Top Chord Compression (F_TChord)
formulas.expression: panel_diagonal_m = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(panel_length_m, 2) + Math.pow(truss_height_m, 2)); theta_rad = Math.atan(truss_height_m / panel_length_m); F_TChord_kn = F_BChord_kn / Math.cos(theta_rad) * (-1)
formulas.reference: Top chord compression = −F_BC / cosθ at mid-span panel; θ = roof pitch angle

formulas.name: Maximum Web Diagonal Force (F_diag) — Near Support
formulas.expression: V_max_kn = R_kn - P_u_kn; F_diag_kn = V_max_kn / Math.sin(theta_rad)
formulas.reference: Maximum shear occurs at first interior panel; F_diag = V_max / sinθ

formulas.name: Required Section Area — Bottom Chord Tension (A_req)
formulas.expression: phi_t = 0.90; A_req_mm2 = F_BChord_kn * 1000 / (phi_t * steel_grade_fy_mpa); wind_uplift_factor = wind_uplift_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m * panel_length_m
formulas.reference: AISC 360-22 D2-1: φP_n = φ_t·F_y·A_g; φ_t=0.90 for yielding; wind uplift checked separately

rules.message: Truss depth-to-span ratio < 1:20: very shallow truss — deflection and chord force will be excessive; minimum h/L = 1/10 recommended per AISC DG11
Wind uplift > 90% of dead load: net uplift possible under 0.9D + 1.0W — bottom chord becomes compression; verify buckling capacity and purlin connections
Less than 4 panels: very steep diagonal angle — excessive web member forces; use minimum 6 panels for efficient truss geometry

smart_warnings.message: Long shallow truss: chord forces very large — chord section and connection design critical; camber ≥ L/500 required per AISC Code of Standard Practice
Top chord compression > 2000 kN: heavy member required — verify lateral buckling between purlin points per AISC 360-22 Chapter E; ensure continuous lateral restraint
High-strength steel on long span: deflection (not strength) may govern — S460 reduces weight but deflection limit L/360 still applies; verify serviceability
Light dead load with heavy imposed load: dynamic amplification possible for crowds or machinery — check vibration criteria per SCI P354 or AISC DG11
Required bottom chord area > 15,000 mm²: very large section — consider HSS, built-up section, or reduce span with intermediate column; verify connection capacity at joints
